item.title: Qatar Says Maritime Navigation in Its Waters to Return Sunday
item.description: Qatar announced the full return of navigation for all maritime vessels and ships from Sunday in its waters, according to its transport ministry.

item.title: RBNZ to Announce Next Steps on Cash-Rate Decision Transparency
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank will soon announce steps to increase the transparency of its monetary policy decisions, Governor Anna Breman said.

item.title: Australia Acts to Secure Urea Amid Supply Risk From Iran War
item.description: Australia set up a government working group with the fertilizer industry to safeguard urea supplies at risk from disruptions linked to the war in Iran, Agriculture Minister Julie Collins said.

item.title: Cameroon Senate Ex-President Dies A Month After He Was Replaced
item.description: Marcel Niat Njifenji, Cameroon’s pioneer senate president who ruled the institution for 13 years, has died one month after he was replaced, Abdoulaye Aboubakary, the new Senate leader said in an emailed statement.

item.title: World Finance Chiefs Head to IMF With a Sense of Déjà Vu
item.description: Economic policymakers are about to gather in Washington to assess the damage President Donald Trump’s war on Iran has caused to growth in the Middle East and beyond.

item.title: AI Juggernaut Rumbles on Even as Markets Whipsaw
item.description: The artificial intelligence credit juggernaut keeps pushing forward as the relentless demand for exposure to the industry trumps fears that the conflict in the Middle East is causing energy prices and inflation to rise.

item.title: The Oil Market Is in the Grip of a Panicked Race for Barrels
item.description: While investors focused on the fragile Iranian ceasefire this week, a desperate scramble for cargoes has been playing out in the oil market, as traders and refiners scour the globe for immediately available supplies.

item.title: Boeing’s Moon Rocket Faces Uncertain Future Under Trump’s NASA
item.description: NASA’s Boeing Co. rocket just propelled astronauts farther into space than ever before. The Trump administration is already looking to competitors for a replacement.

item.title: Zambia’s Cabinet Approves Revised 2026 Budget as Fuel Costs Jump
item.description: Zambia’s cabinet has approved a revised budget for 2026 to address revenue pressures arising from the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: US Navy Ships Crossed Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, Axios Says
item.description: Several US navy ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday in a move that wasn’t coordinated with Iran, Axios reported, citing a US official it didn’t identify.

item.title: Chokepoints in the Strait of Hormuz and in Producing Critical Minerals
item.description: There’s a lot at stake.

item.title: War Diversion Sparks 40% More Ships Refueling in Mauritius Port
item.description: The island nation of Mauritius has become an increasingly popular refueling stop for commercial cargo vessels avoiding the Middle East due to the Iran war.

item.title: Why China’s New ‘Defining Moment’ Isn’t About Stimulus
item.description: Moving toward a data economy means money for technology firms, not consumers.

item.title: Bank of England Set to Discuss Anthropic’s Mythos With Banks
item.description: The Bank of England plans to discuss the impact of Anthropic PBC’s new AI model with financial institutions, as UK regulators join their peers in the US and elsewhere in raising alarms over the risks posed by the tool.

item.title: Three Oil Supertankers Appear to Make Move Through Hormuz Strait
item.description: Two Chinese supertankers loaded with crude appeared to be transiting the Strait of Hormuz hours after a Greek vessel moved through the waterway, in what would mark a significant uptick in oil shipping traffic days after a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran was announced.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: War Fuels Inflation, Saps Sentiment
item.description: Surging fuel costs stemming from the Iran war are sparking a rapid run-up in inflation and sinking consumer confidence.

item.title: Europe Energy Traders Brace for 21-Hour Day as Volatility Surges
item.description: European gas and power markets, once a niche corner of global energy trading, are in for a major change next week: Hours will more than double to 21 from 10, ending the narrow daytime window that has been the norm for years.

item.title: The Big Macro Force That's Been Driving Stocks Higher for Years
item.description: How inequality has helped keep the stock market boom alive.

item.title: Odd Lots: The Big Macro Force That’s Kept Stocks High (Podcast)
item.description: Stocks have gone up over the years because corporate earnings continue to grow. That part is straightforward. But in addition to rising stock prices, we’ve also seen rising stock market valuations. For years, investors have talked about stocks being unreasonably priced, and yet they haven’t reverted to historical norms. But perhaps there’s a good explanation for this, beyond just animal spirits. Jonathan Heathcote is an economist at the Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank, who recently co-authored

item.title: Mexico Central Bank Taps Heffner to be New Chief Economist
item.description: Mexico’s central bank has tapped Aldo Heffner Rodriguez as its new chief economist, filling a role that has been vacant for half a year.

item.title: OpenAI Accuses Musk of ‘Ambush’ as $100 Billion-Plus Trial Looms
item.description: OpenAI says Elon Musk has suddenly changed direction on what he’s seeking in his lawsuit against the startup in a “legal ambush” just weeks before trial.

item.title: Starmer Plots Higher UK Defense Spending in Bid to Save His Job
item.description: Keir Starmer has resolved to hike Britain’s defense spending faster than current plans propose, hoping that a focus on security at a time of escalating conflict can fend off the prospect of a leadership challenge next month.

item.title: Japan Bets $16 Billion to Propel Startup Rapidus Into AI Chips
item.description: Japan approved ¥631.5 billion ($4 billion) in additional subsidies to quicken Rapidus Corp. into the intensely competitive AI chipmaking arena, ramping up financial support for a signature project widely regarded as a long shot.

item.title: Global Funds Flee Indian Stocks at Record Pace on Growth Fears
item.description: Global funds are dumping Indian equities at a record clip as an energy shock from the US-Iran war threatens to derail the outlook of the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

item.title: IOI Properties Files REIT Listing That May Raise $500 Million
item.description: IOI Properties Group Bhd. plans to establish a real estate investment trust holding its retail, hotel, and office assets, followed by a listing that could raise about 1.98 billion ringgit ($500 million) for the Malaysian developer.

item.title: Kalshi Wins Reprieve From Arizona Criminal Case at CFTC’s Urging
item.description: Kalshi Inc. won a temporary reprieve from criminal prosecution in Arizona when a judge suspended the case at the behest of a federal regulator.

item.title: Apple Hires Uber’s Asia-Pacific Government Relations Chief
item.description: Apple Inc. has hired Uber Technologies Inc.’s head of public policy and government relations for Asia-Pacific as it looks to revamp its supply chains in the region.

item.title: Tiger Global Backs PopUp Bagels at $300 Million Valuation
item.description: Tiger Global has agreed to invest in PopUp Bagels at a $300 million valuation, according to people familiar with the matter — roughly five times what the startup was quoted at as recently as five months ago.

item.title: Fed Seeks Details on US Banks’ Exposure to Private Credit Firms
item.description: The Federal Reserve is asking major US banks for details about their exposure to private credit following a surge in redemptions from the funds and a rise in troubled loans in the industry, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: PJM Targets 15 Gigawatts of New Power for Data Center Boom
item.description: PJM Interconnection LLC is seeking 15 gigawatts of new power supplies in an emergency proposal to address potential electricity shortages stemming from the boom in artificial intelligence.
